objectives: To introduce key vocabulary related to leisure activities.
To practice listening and reading comprehension through dialogues.
To enhance speaking skills by discussing personal preferences and leisure activities.
To use expressions of preference and invitations in context.
materials: Audio track of the conversation between Tom and Trang.
Printed copies of the conversation for each student.
Images for activity 3 [IMAGE_TAG_17].
Whiteboard and markers.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To engage students and introduce the topic of leisure activities.
stages.target_language: DIY, leisure time
stages.content: Teacher introduces the topic of leisure activities and asks students about their favorite hobbies.

activities.action: Greet the class and ask, 'What do you like to do in your free time?' Encourage students to share their hobbies.
Respond to the teacher’s question and share their favorite leisure activities.

stages.name: Stage 1: Listen and Read
stages.aim: To practice listening and reading comprehension.
stages.target_language: Knitting kit, keen on, leisure time, hang out, cinema, comedy
stages.content: Students listen to and read the conversation between Tom and Trang.

activities.action: Play the audio track of the conversation. Ask students to listen and follow along with their printed copies.
Listen to the audio and read the conversation silently.

stages.name: Stage 2: Complete the Sentences
stages.aim: To check comprehension of the conversation.
stages.target_language: knitting kit, leisure time, his friends, hang out, Sunday
stages.content: Students complete sentences based on the conversation.

activities.action: Distribute the worksheet with incomplete sentences. Instruct students to fill in the blanks using information from the conversation.
Complete the sentences individually, then compare answers with a partner.

stages.name: Stage 3: Work in Pairs
stages.aim: To practice identifying activities from descriptions.
stages.target_language: doing DIY, doing puzzles, surfing the net, cooking, playing sport, messaging friends
stages.content: Students match activities to pictures.

activities.action: Display [IMAGE_TAG_17] on the board. Explain that students need to match the activities to the correct pictures.
Work in pairs to discuss and write the correct activity under each picture.
[IMAGE_TAG_17]

stages.name: Stage 4: Guess the Activities
stages.aim: To practice inferring activities from descriptions.
stages.target_language: improve memory, a mental exercise, save money, increase creativity, improve physical health, make friends, keep in touch, be relaxed, learn IT, computer skills
stages.content: Students guess activities from given phrases.

activities.action: Read each phrase aloud and ask students to guess which activity it describes.
Discuss in pairs and write down their guesses.

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion
stages.aim: To practice speaking by discussing plans for free time.
stages.target_language: If you have some free time this weekend, what will you do?
stages.content: Students ask and answer questions about their weekend plans.

activities.action: Divide students into small groups. Instruct them to ask each other the question: 'If you have some free time this weekend, what will you do?'.
Discuss in groups and note down each member’s response.
Report their group’s answers to the class.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: DIY, knitting kit, leisure time, hang out, cinema, comedy
stages.content: Teacher reviews key vocabulary and expressions learned in the lesson.

activities.action: Ask students to recall the new vocabulary and expressions they learned today. Write them on the board.
Volunteer to share what they remember from the lesson.

objectives: To introduce and practice vocabulary related to likes and dislikes.
To use expressions of likes and dislikes in sentences.
To distinguish between the sounds /ʊ/ and /u:/ in pronunciation.
To enhance listening and speaking skills through practice activities.
materials: Printed copies of the vocabulary matching exercise for each student.
Audio track for pronunciation of /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds.
Printed sentences for pronunciation practice.
Whiteboard and markers.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of likes and dislikes.
stages.target_language: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into
stages.content: Teacher introduces expressions of likes and dislikes using 'be' and prepositions.

activities.action: Write the expressions 'be fond of', 'be keen on', 'be interested in', 'be crazy about', 'be into' on the board. Ask students what these phrases might mean.
Share their ideas about the meanings of the expressions.

stages.name: Stage 1: Vocabulary Matching
stages.aim: To practice matching words and phrases to form expressions about likes.
stages.target_language: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into
stages.content: Students match words from column A with words from column B to form expressions.

activities.action: Distribute the vocabulary matching exercise. Instruct students to match words from column A with column B.
[IMAGE_TAG_30]
Work individually to match the words and phrases, then check answers with a partner.

stages.name: Stage 2: Fill in the Blanks
stages.aim: To reinforce vocabulary by filling in blanks with correct words.
stages.target_language: fond, interested, keen, into, crazy
stages.content: Students fill in blanks in sentences using words from the box.

activities.action: Provide students with the sentences and the word box. Instruct them to fill in the blanks.
[IMAGE_TAG_33]
Complete the sentences individually, then compare answers in pairs.

stages.name: Stage 3: Sentence Completion
stages.aim: To practice using expressions of likes and dislikes in sentences.
stages.target_language: I'm crazy about, I'm keen on, I'm fond of, I'm not interested in, I'm not into
stages.content: Students complete sentences about their own likes and dislikes.

activities.action: Explain the task and provide example: 'I'm interested in doing DIY.'
[IMAGE_TAG_41]
Complete the sentences using words or phrases from the box or their own ideas.

stages.name: Stage 4: Pronunciation Practice
stages.aim: To practice distinguishing and pronouncing /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds.
stages.target_language: /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds
stages.content: Students listen and repeat words, sorting them into /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds.

activities.action: Play the audio track for the pronunciation of words. Instruct students to repeat after each word.
[IMAGE_TAG_51]
[IMAGE_TAG_52]
Repeat the words and sort them into the correct columns on their worksheets.

stages.name: Stage 5: Sentence Practice
stages.aim: To practice pronunciation in context by identifying /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds in sentences.
stages.target_language: /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds in context
stages.content: Students listen to sentences and mark the /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds.

activities.action: Read the sentences aloud. Ask students to underline words with /ʊ/ and circle words with /u:/.
Listen carefully and mark the sounds as instructed.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review vocabulary and pronunciation learned in the lesson.
stages.target_language: be fond of, be keen on, be interested in, be crazy about, be into, /ʊ/ and /u:/ sounds
stages.content: Teacher reviews key vocabulary and sounds from the lesson.

activities.action: Ask students to recall the expressions and sounds they practiced. Write examples on the board.
Volunteer to share sentences using the new vocabulary and practice the sounds.

objectives: To understand and use verbs of liking and disliking with gerunds and to-infinitives.
To categorize verbs based on their grammatical usage.
To practice choosing the correct form of verbs in sentences.
To enhance speaking skills through interactive activities.
materials: Printed copies of the verb categorization exercise for each student.
Printed sentences for the multiple-choice exercise.
Pictures for sentence completion activity [IMAGE_TAG_76].
Printed templates for personal sentence completion [IMAGE_TAG_79].
Whiteboard and markers.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of verbs of liking and disliking.
stages.target_language: love, like, enjoy, fancy, prefer, dislike, detest, hate
stages.content: Teacher introduces verbs of liking and disliking and their usage with gerunds and to-infinitives.

activities.action: Write the verbs 'love', 'like', 'enjoy', 'fancy', 'prefer', 'dislike', 'detest', 'hate' on the board. Ask students how they think these verbs are used in sentences.
Share their ideas about the usage of these verbs.

stages.name: Stage 1: Verb Categorization
stages.aim: To categorize verbs based on their grammatical usage with gerunds and to-infinitives.
stages.target_language: Verbs followed by gerunds only, Verbs followed by both gerunds and to-infinitives
stages.content: Students categorize verbs into two columns based on their usage with gerunds and to-infinitives.

activities.action: Distribute the verb categorization exercise. Instruct students to work in pairs to categorize the verbs.
[IMAGE_TAG_64]
Work in pairs to categorize the verbs and discuss their answers.

stages.name: Stage 2: Multiple-Choice Exercise
stages.aim: To practice choosing the correct form of verbs in sentences.
stages.target_language: travelling, doing, to watch, going, hunting
stages.content: Students choose the correct answer for each sentence from options A, B, or C.

activities.action: Provide students with printed sentences and multiple-choice options. Instruct them to choose the correct answer.
Complete the exercise individually, then compare answers with a partner.

stages.name: Stage 3: Sentence Completion with Pictures
stages.aim: To complete sentences using verbs in their suitable form based on pictures.
stages.target_language: like, enjoy, dislike, hate, prefer
stages.content: Students complete sentences using verbs in brackets, with reference to pictures.

activities.action: Display [IMAGE_TAG_76] and explain the task. Ask students to complete the sentences using the verbs in brackets.
Work individually to complete the sentences, then discuss answers in pairs.
[IMAGE_TAG_76]

stages.name: Stage 4: Personal Sentence Completion
stages.aim: To practice expressing personal likes and dislikes using verbs of liking and disliking.
stages.target_language: I like, I prefer, I love, I dislike, I hate, I detest
stages.content: Students complete sentences about themselves using verbs of liking and disliking.

activities.action: Distribute printed templates [IMAGE_TAG_79] for students to fill in with their personal preferences.
Complete the sentences individually and share with a partner.

stages.name: Stage 5: Likes and Dislikes Mimes Game
stages.aim: To practice using verbs of liking and disliking in an interactive game.
stages.target_language: Do you like, Do you love
stages.content: Students play a game where they mime a leisure activity they like or dislike, and others guess by asking Yes/No questions.

activities.action: Explain the rules of the game and provide an example dialogue.
Work in groups to play the game, taking turns to mime and guess activities.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: love, like, enjoy, fancy, prefer, dislike, detest, hate
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points from the lesson and checks understanding.

activities.action: Ask students to recall the verbs and their usage. Write examples on the board.
Volunteer to share what they learned and ask any remaining questions.

objectives: To practice inviting and accepting invitations using everyday English expressions.
To identify leisure activities from images and texts.
To understand and summarize information about teenagers' leisure activities around the world.
To engage in group discussions about personal preferences for leisure activities.
materials: Audio recording of dialogues for listening practice.
Printed copies of dialogues for each student.
Images for activity identification [IMAGE_TAG_82], [IMAGE_TAG_85].
Printed text excerpts for reading activity [IMAGE_TAG_88].
Table template for completing information [IMAGE_TAG_89].
Whiteboard and markers.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of inviting and accepting invitations.
stages.target_language: Would you like to...?, Do you fancy...?, I’d love to., That’s great.
stages.content: Teacher introduces expressions for inviting and accepting invitations.

activities.action: Write the expressions 'Would you like to...?', 'Do you fancy...?', 'I’d love to.', 'That’s great.' on the board. Ask students when they might use these expressions.
Discuss in pairs and share their ideas with the class.

stages.name: Stage 1: Listen and Read Dialogues
stages.aim: To practice listening and reading comprehension using dialogues.
stages.target_language: Would you like to...?, I’d love to., Do you fancy...?, That’s great.
stages.content: Students listen to and read dialogues focusing on inviting and accepting invitations.

activities.action: Play the audio recording of the dialogues. Ask students to listen and follow along in their printed copies.
[IMAGE_TAG_82]
Listen to the dialogues and read along, paying attention to the highlighted expressions.

stages.name: Stage 2: Practice Inviting and Accepting Invitations
stages.aim: To practice using inviting and accepting expressions in various situations.
stages.target_language: Would you like to play badminton?, Do you fancy making paper flowers?, I’d love to try your home-made pizza.
stages.content: Students practice dialogues in pairs using the highlighted language.

activities.action: Instruct students to work in pairs and practice inviting and accepting invitations for the given situations.
Role-play the dialogues in pairs, taking turns to invite and accept.

stages.name: Stage 3: Identify Activities from Pictures
stages.aim: To identify and describe leisure activities from images.
stages.target_language: Activity identification
stages.content: Students work in pairs to identify activities in pictures.

activities.action: Display [IMAGE_TAG_85] and ask students to discuss what each activity might be.
Work in pairs to identify and describe the activities in the images.
[IMAGE_TAG_85]

stages.name: Stage 4: Read and Complete the Table
stages.aim: To read and extract information about leisure activities from the text.
stages.target_language: Origami, Snowboarding, Leisure activity, Benefits of the activity
stages.content: Students read about teenagers' leisure activities and complete a table with the information.

activities.action: Distribute the text excerpts [IMAGE_TAG_88] and table template [IMAGE_TAG_89]. Instruct students to read and fill in the table.
Read the texts and complete the table with the required information.
[IMAGE_TAG_88]
[IMAGE_TAG_89]

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion
stages.aim: To discuss personal preferences for leisure activities and practice speaking skills.
stages.target_language: Which activity do you want to try?, Why do you want to try it?
stages.content: Students discuss in groups which activities they would like to try and why.

activities.action: Ask students to form groups and discuss the questions provided. Instruct them to prepare to report their group's answers.
Discuss in groups and prepare a short report on their group's preferences.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review key expressions and summarize the lesson.
stages.target_language: Would you like to...?, I’d love to., Leisure activities
stages.content: Teacher reviews the key expressions and activities discussed in the lesson.

activities.action: Ask students to recall the expressions for inviting and accepting invitations. Summarize the activities identified and discussed.
Volunteer to share what they learned and enjoyed about the lesson.

objectives: To identify and discuss various leisure activities.
To improve reading comprehension through detailed text analysis.
To practice speaking skills by discussing personal leisure activities with family.
To enhance vocabulary related to leisure activities and family interactions.
materials: Printed copies of the reading text for each student.
Images for activity identification [IMAGE_TAG_97].
Printed tables for recording group answers [IMAGE_TAG_103].
Whiteboard and markers.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities
stages.content: Teacher introduces the concept of leisure activities and asks students about their favorite activities.

activities.action: Ask students, 'What are some activities you do in your free time?' Write responses on the board.
Share their favorite leisure activities with the class.

stages.name: Stage 1: Picture Identification
stages.aim: To identify leisure activities from pictures.
stages.target_language: Bike ride, Cooking, DIY
stages.content: Students look at pictures and identify the activities they see.

activities.action: Display [IMAGE_TAG_97] and ask students to describe what activities they see.
Discuss in pairs and identify the activities in the pictures.
[IMAGE_TAG_97]

stages.name: Stage 2: Reading Comprehension - Multiple Choice
stages.aim: To improve comprehension by answering multiple-choice questions about the text.
stages.target_language: Connect, Bike ride, Cooking, DIY
stages.content: Students read about Trang's leisure activities and answer multiple-choice questions.

activities.action: Distribute the reading text and ask students to read silently.
Read the text and choose the correct answers for the questions provided.

stages.name: Stage 3: Vocabulary Meaning
stages.aim: To understand the meaning of specific vocabulary in context.
stages.target_language: Connect
stages.content: Students identify the meaning of the word 'connect' in the context of the text.

activities.action: Ask students, 'What does the word "connect" mean in the text?' Provide options A, B, C.
Discuss in pairs and choose the option they think is correct.

stages.name: Stage 4: Detailed Reading and Question Answering
stages.aim: To answer detailed questions about the text to enhance understanding.
stages.target_language: Bike ride, Recipes, Ingredients, DIY, Costume contest
stages.content: Students read the text again and answer detailed questions.

activities.action: Provide students with the questions and ask them to find answers in the text.
Work individually to answer the questions, then compare answers in pairs.

stages.name: Stage 5: Group Discussion - Leisure Activities
stages.aim: To practice speaking about personal leisure activities with family.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, Feelings, Preferences
stages.content: Students discuss their own leisure activities with family in groups and record answers.

activities.action: Form groups and distribute the table template [IMAGE_TAG_103]. Instruct students to ask and answer the questions in their groups.
Take turns asking and answering the questions, recording their friends' answers in the table.
[IMAGE_TAG_103]

stages.name: Stage 6: Reporting Group Findings
stages.aim: To report and discuss common leisure activities within the class.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities
stages.content: Students report their group's findings to the class and discuss common activities.

activities.action: Ask each group to report their findings. Discuss which activities are most common.
Present their group's answers and participate in the class discussion.

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, Connect, DIY, Preferences
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points from the lesson and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the main activities discussed and ask students to share what they learned.
Reflect on the lesson and ask any remaining questions.

objectives: To practice listening skills through an interview about leisure activities.
To enhance comprehension by filling in information from an audio text.
To develop speaking skills by discussing personal leisure activities with peers.
To improve writing skills by composing an email about personal leisure activities.
materials: Audio recording of the interview with Mark.
Printed tables for filling in information from the audio [IMAGE_TAG_121].
Printed email templates for writing practice [IMAGE_TAG_128].
Whiteboard and markers.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the topic of leisure activities and engage students.
stages.target_language: Leisure time, Activities
stages.content: Teacher introduces the concept of leisure activities with friends and asks students about their experiences.

activities.action: Ask students, 'What are some activities you enjoy doing with your friends during your free time?' Write responses on the board.
Share their experiences and favorite activities with the class.

stages.name: Stage 1: Pre-listening Discussion
stages.aim: To prepare students for listening by discussing related questions.
stages.target_language: Leisure time, Activities, Friends
stages.content: Students discuss questions about leisure activities with friends.

activities.action: Pose the questions: 'In your opinion, what activities can we do with our friends in our leisure time?' and 'Why should we spend time with our friends?'.
Discuss the questions in pairs and share their ideas with the class.

stages.name: Stage 2: Listening for Specific Information
stages.aim: To practice listening for specific information by answering multiple-choice questions.
stages.target_language: Free time, Family, Friends
stages.content: Students listen to an interview with Mark and choose the correct answers.

activities.action: Play the audio recording of the interview. Ask students to listen and choose the correct answers from the options provided.
Listen to the audio and select the correct answers for the questions.

stages.name: Stage 3: Listening and Filling in Information
stages.aim: To enhance listening comprehension by filling in blanks with information from the audio.
stages.target_language: Cooking, Volleyball, Skateboard, Fun
stages.content: Students listen again and fill in the blanks in a table with no more than two words.

activities.action: Play the audio recording again. Instruct students to fill in the blanks in the table provided.
Listen to the audio and complete the table with the missing information.
[IMAGE_TAG_121]

stages.name: Stage 4: Pair Work - Speaking Practice
stages.aim: To practice speaking skills by asking and answering questions about personal leisure activities.
stages.target_language: Free time, Activities, Friends
stages.content: Students work in pairs to ask and answer questions about their free time activities.

activities.action: Provide students with the questions and ask them to discuss in pairs.
Take turns asking and answering the questions, noting down their partner's responses.

stages.name: Stage 5: Writing an Email
stages.aim: To practice writing skills by composing an email about personal leisure activities.
stages.target_language: Email structure, Free time, Activities
stages.content: Students write an email to a penfriend about their leisure activities using the answers from the speaking task.

activities.action: Distribute the email template [IMAGE_TAG_128] and instruct students to write an email using their answers from the previous task.
Compose an email following the template and share it with a partner for feedback.
[IMAGE_TAG_128]

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, Email writing
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points from the lesson and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the main activities discussed and ask students to share what they learned.
Reflect on the lesson and ask any remaining questions.

objectives: To review and consolidate vocabulary related to leisure activities.
To practice forming complete sentences from cues.
To reinforce the correct use of gerunds after verbs of liking and disliking.
To enhance writing skills by completing sentences and passages using correct verb forms.
materials: Printed worksheets with sentence completion exercises.
Printed cue sentences for sentence formation practice.
Audio recordings for listening exercises (if applicable).
Whiteboard and markers.

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To engage students and introduce the topic of leisure activities.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, Online, Puzzles, DIY
stages.content: Teacher introduces the topic of leisure activities and asks students to share their favorite activities.

activities.action: Ask students, 'What are some leisure activities you enjoy doing in your free time?' Write responses on the board.
Share their favorite leisure activities with the class.

stages.name: Stage 1: Vocabulary Review - Sentence Completion
stages.aim: To review vocabulary by completing sentences with appropriate leisure activities.
stages.target_language: Online, Puzzles, DIY, Messaging, Football, Internet
stages.content: Students complete sentences using appropriate leisure activities.

activities.action: Distribute worksheets with sentence completion exercises. Instruct students to fill in the blanks with suitable leisure activities.
Complete the sentences individually, then compare answers with a partner.

stages.name: Stage 2: Sentence Formation from Cues
stages.aim: To practice forming complete sentences from given cues.
stages.target_language: Crazy about, Interested in, Not fond of, Not into, Keen on
stages.content: Students write complete sentences using provided cues.

activities.action: Provide students with cue sentences and ask them to form complete sentences.
Write complete sentences individually, then share with a partner for feedback.

stages.name: Stage 3: Grammar Practice - Gerunds
stages.aim: To reinforce the correct use of gerunds after verbs of liking and disliking.
stages.target_language: Enjoy cycling, Don't like reading, Detests playing, Love chatting, Prefer doing
stages.content: Students fill in blanks with correct forms of verbs in brackets.

activities.action: Distribute worksheets with sentences requiring correct verb forms. Instruct students to fill in the blanks with gerunds.
Complete the sentences individually and check answers with a partner.

stages.name: Stage 4: Passage Completion
stages.aim: To apply understanding of verb forms by completing a passage.
stages.target_language: Ride, Read, Message, Make, Knit, Dislikes playing
stages.content: Students complete a passage using correct verb forms and additional words.

activities.action: Provide the passage and pictures [IMAGE_TAG_151]. Instruct students to complete the passage using the correct verb forms.
Work individually to complete the passage, then discuss answers in pairs.
[IMAGE_TAG_151]

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the lesson and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, Gerunds, Sentence formation
stages.content: Teacher reviews key points from the lesson and addresses any questions.

activities.action: Summarize the main activities and grammar points discussed. Ask students to share what they learned.
Reflect on the lesson and ask any remaining questions.

objectives: To conduct interviews to gather information about leisure activities.
To practice using questions to gather information about preferences and activities.
To summarize and report findings about leisure activities to the class.
To use vocabulary and expressions related to leisure activities, likes, and dislikes.
materials: Printed interview question sheets for each group.
Printed tables for collecting answers [IMAGE_TAG_164].
Whiteboard and markers for reporting findings.
Printed criteria checklist for reporting [IMAGE_TAG_172].

stages.name: Warm-up
stages.aim: To introduce the project and engage students in discussing leisure activities.
stages.target_language: Leisure time, Free time, Activities, Interview
stages.content: Teacher introduces the project and asks students about their favorite leisure activities.

activities.action: Explain the project task: to conduct interviews about leisure activities. Ask students, 'What are some of your favorite activities to do in your free time?' Write responses on the board.
Share their favorite leisure activities with the class.

stages.name: Stage 1: Preparing for the Interview
stages.aim: To prepare students for conducting interviews by reviewing the questions.
stages.target_language: When do you have free time?, What do you like doing with your family?, What do you like doing on your own?
stages.content: Students review the interview questions and prepare to conduct interviews.

activities.action: Distribute the interview questions and review them with the class. Ensure students understand each question.
Read through the questions and discuss in pairs to clarify any doubts.

stages.name: Stage 2: Conducting the Interviews
stages.aim: To gather information about leisure activities by interviewing classmates.
stages.target_language: Free time, Family, Friends, Own, Activities
stages.content: Students conduct interviews with classmates using the prepared questions.

activities.action: Divide the class into groups and assign each group to interview a set number of classmates. Provide printed tables for recording answers [IMAGE_TAG_164].
Work in groups to interview classmates and record their answers in the provided table.
[IMAGE_TAG_164]

stages.name: Stage 3: Analyzing and Summarizing Data
stages.aim: To analyze and summarize the data collected from interviews.
stages.target_language: Popular activities, Most common, Would like to try
stages.content: Students analyze the data collected and prepare to report their findings.

activities.action: Instruct groups to analyze their data and identify common themes and popular activities.
Work in groups to discuss the data and prepare a summary of their findings.

stages.name: Stage 4: Reporting Findings
stages.aim: To report the findings of the leisure time survey to the class.
stages.target_language: How many students, Popular activities, Would like to try
stages.content: Students report their findings to the class using guiding questions and criteria.

activities.action: Ask each group to report their findings to the class. Use the guiding questions to structure the reports.
Present their group's findings to the class, addressing each guiding question.
[IMAGE_TAG_172]

stages.name: Wrap-up
stages.aim: To review the project and consolidate learning.
stages.target_language: Leisure activities, Likes and dislikes, Reporting
stages.content: Teacher reviews the project and discusses what students learned about leisure activities.

activities.action: Summarize the main findings from the reports and ask students to reflect on what they learned about leisure activities.
Reflect on the project and share any new insights or interesting findings.
